diff_aware_contract: Mechanism (ii) — inspector-side tagging. Every finding tagged with introduced_in_diff:bool. Absent positive marker = default-safe ignore per AC-2.6 (b).

advisory_observations.observation: README claims 'Total files: 1' but directory contains 11 entries. INDEX.md (also auto-generated) is correct with full 11-entry tree. This is doc-sync hook metadata drift — README will refresh on next hook run.
advisory_observations.rationale: Auto-generated content drift, not a misplaced/orphaned/duplicate file issue. Hook-managed; self-healing on next sync.

advisory_observations.observation: Local pytest bytecode cache exists but is gitignored (root .gitignore line 'pycache' rule covers __pycache__/). Not part of the committed diff.
advisory_observations.rationale: Properly excluded from VCS; no cleanup action required.

checks_performed.misplaced_docs: all .md files in correct locations (tests/generated/<task>/INDEX.md and README.md follow standard folder convention)
checks_performed.naming_violations: test files follow test_AC_NN_<uid>.py pattern consistent with peer task directories under tests/generated/
checks_performed.unreferenced_scripts: scripts/score-update.sh verified via check-file-references.sh — has 3+ doc references (scripts/README.md, scripts/INDEX.md, root INDEX.md) plus usage in commands/close.md, commands/dev.md, agents/changelog-analyst.md
checks_performed.orphaned_tests: all 10 generated tests target existing source files (scripts/score-update.sh, commands/close.md, agents/changelog-analyst.md) — verified via manifest.json hook_check.file entries
checks_performed.non_functional_files: no temp/backup/build artifact files in changed-files scope; __pycache__/ is gitignored
checks_performed.duplicate_content: no byte-identical duplicates among 10 test files (each targets distinct AC)
checks_performed.obsolete_functionality: no superseded code introduced in diff; all 3 source-file modifications are forward-evolution edits
checks_performed.archive_candidates: all changed files modified <1 day, actively used in current cycle — no archive triggers

verdict.rationale: Zero blocking cleanliness violations introduced in this diff. Two advisory observations (auto-generated README staleness, gitignored pycache) are non-blocking per Section 5.4 rule 3 cleanliness scope and AC-2.6 default-safe ignore rule. Pre-existing cross-session uncommitted files (~150) are out of scope per --changed-files contract.

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex confirmed CLOSE: YES verdict. No blocking false negatives found in --changed-files cleanliness scope. Codex independently identified the same two OBSERVATION_ONLY items (README staleness, gitignored pycache) and concurred neither should block close — README drift is generated-doc metadata, not a misplaced/orphaned/duplicate issue.

findings.proposed_fix: rerun doc-sync/regenerate README, or allow next hook run to refresh
findings.rationale: Auto-generated hook-managed metadata drift. Self-healing on next sync. Not a cleanliness-scope violation (not misplaced/orphaned/duplicate). Logged as advisory_observations[0].

findings.proposed_fix: optional cleanup if requiring pristine workspace
findings.rationale: Gitignored, not part of committed diff. Logged as advisory_observations[1].

codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: Both codex observations already captured as advisory_observations with blocking:false. Verdict CLOSE: YES preserved.
